finding: An aneurysm is defined as a vessel segment with a diameter of > 150% of the diameter of a normal

differentials.name: Spontaneous occlusion of aorta Prognosis:  17% 5-year survival without surgery, 50-60% 5-year survival with surgery
differentials.raw: Spontaneous occlusion of aorta Prognosis:  17% 5-year survival without surgery, 50-60% 5-year survival with surgery

differentials.name: Excision of aneurysm + aortic interposition graft
differentials.raw: Excision of aneurysm + aortic interposition graft (4-5% surgical mortality for nonruptured, 30-80% for ruptured aneurysm)

context: adjacent segment + involving < 50% of the total vessel length! Cause: ascending aorta Reiter syndrome, psoriasis, ulcerative colitis, systemic lupus erythematosus, scleroderma, Behçet disease, ulcerative colitis, radiation Pathophysiology: intimal injury of aortic wall → lipid-laden macrophage + lymphocyte infiltration → inflammatory cells secrete cytokines → activation of multiple proteolytic cascades → degradation of collagen + elastin within tunica media → weakening + dilatation of aortic wall Location:   abdominal aorta (31%), ascending aorta (22%), arch (12%), descending thoracic aorta (8
